question: Based on these maps, what can be concluded about overall population density levels in urban vs rural areas across U.S. states?

options.A: It cannot be determined from these maps alone
options.B: Rural areas clearly have higher overall population density than urban areas
options.C: Both urban and rural areas have about the same overall population density levels
options.D: Urban areas clearly have higher overall population density than rural areas

explanation: Both maps reuse the same threshold scale, but their underlying distributions differ. This can make one map appear more intense simply because more states fall into the top bin. In the underlying data, Urban Population Density has the higher average value, so that is the only reliable conclusion.
